reforms.description: The UK government has implemented permanent full expensing of investment since April 2023, allowing businesses to deduct the full cost of eligible investments from their taxable income in the year the investment is made.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This reform encourages businesses to invest in new capital, which can enhance productivity and drive economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been in effect since April 2023.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the corporate tax system, promoting investment and potentially leading to substantial increases in business capital and productivity.
reforms.source_quote: The permanent full expensing of investment has been implemented since April 2023.

reforms.description: The UK government aims to enhance business investment to improve productivity by fostering a conducive environment through changes in planning regulation, tax policies, and access to finance for smaller businesses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving investment conditions, the reform is expected to raise productivity and efficiency in the labor market, leading to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has outlined several steps to support business investment.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in policy aimed at addressing long-standing issues of low investment and productivity, which are critical for the UK's economic future.
reforms.source_quote: Given fiscal constraints, it is crucial to foster a conducive environment for business investment.

reforms.description: The UK government has increased Research and Development (R&D) support as part of its efforts to boost business investment and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D support is expected to enhance innovation capabilities, leading to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has taken steps to increase R&D support as part of its investment strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing the UK's innovation landscape, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The government has increased Research and Development (R&D) support to boost business investment.

reforms.description: The UK government has replaced the temporary 'super-deduction' with a 'full expensing' policy, allowing businesses to deduct 100% of the cost of qualifying new plant and machinery investments from taxable income.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This policy is designed to incentivize businesses to invest in new equipment, thereby enhancing productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The super-deduction was replaced with full expensing from April 2023.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a major shift in the corporate tax policy aimed at stimulating business investment and enhancing productivity.
reforms.source_quote: From April 2023, the super-deduction was replaced with full expensing, which offers 100% capital allowances on qualifying new plant and machinery investments.

reforms.description: The UK government has made full expensing for qualifying new plant and machinery investments permanent, which allows businesses to immediately deduct the full cost from their taxable profits. This reform is expected to increase business investment by approximately GBP 3 billion annually, enhancing potential GDP in the long term.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing the cost of capital for businesses, this reform encourages investment in new technologies and equipment, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Full expensing was made permanent in Autumn 2023.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the corporate tax system, aimed at enhancing business investment and productivity across the economy.
reforms.source_quote: Since April 2023, incorporated businesses subject to UK corporation tax can immediately deduct the full cost of qualifying new plant and machinery investment under full expensing.

reforms.description: The British Business Bank has been instrumental in improving access to finance for small and high-growth potential firms, supporting over GBP 12 billion of finance through its programmes since its establishment in 2014.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating access to finance, this initiative enables small firms to invest in innovation and growth, which is crucial for enhancing overall economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The Bank supported about GBP 12 billion of finance through its programmes, assisting over 96,000 businesses by 2022.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative significantly enhances the financial ecosystem for small businesses, promoting innovation and growth in the sector.
reforms.source_quote: The British Business Bank, set up with the objective to restructure UK’s finance markets to better serve small businesses in 2014, supported about GBP 12 billion of finance through its programmes.

reforms.description: The UK government announced the extension of the British Patient Capital’s mandate until 2034 and provided the 'Future Fund: Breakthrough' programme with at least GBP 50 million additional funding to support high-growth, innovative firms focused on R&D-intensive projects.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This funding aims to accelerate the deployment of breakthrough technologies, fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government provided additional funding to the Future Fund: Breakthrough programme.
reforms.importance_rationale: The extension of the Future Fund: Breakthrough programme represents a significant commitment to support innovative firms, enhancing the UK's startup ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: It is welcome that the government announced the extension of the British Patient Capital’s mandate until 2034 and provided the 'Future Fund: Breakthrough' programme with at least GBP 50 million additional funding.

reforms.description: The UK government is considering allowing the British Business Bank to borrow capital through the issuance of government guaranteed bonds to scale up its operations, which would enhance access to finance for innovative firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This proposal aims to improve access to finance for innovative firms, thereby fostering entrepreneurship and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government could consider allowing the Bank to borrow capital through the issuance of government guaranteed bonds.
reforms.importance_rationale: This proposal represents a significant potential change in how the British Business Bank could operate, enhancing its capacity to support innovative firms.
reforms.source_quote: The government could consider allowing the Bank to borrow capital through the issuance of government guaranteed bonds.

reforms.description: The UK government has laid out a broad strategy for decarbonising the housing sector, which includes explicit emission reduction commitments and a focus on electrification of heat in buildings via heat pumps and heat networks.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The strategy aims to reduce greenhouse gas emissions, improve energy efficiency, and lower heating costs, contributing to sustainable economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government clarified its strategy and commitments in October 2023.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in policy aimed at achieving net zero emissions in the housing sector, which is crucial for the UK's overall climate goals.
reforms.source_quote: The UK government has laid out a broad strategy for decarbonising the housing sector, with explicit emission reduction commitments for the building sector.

reforms.description: The UK government plans to tighten energy efficiency standards for rental housing to a minimum Energy Performance Certificate (EPC) rating of C by 2028, which was previously scrapped. This aims to improve the overall energy efficiency of the housing stock and reduce emissions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving energy efficiency standards can lead to lower energy consumption and emissions, supporting the transition to a low-carbon economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to phase in higher minimum performance standards for rental housing.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant policy shift aimed at improving energy efficiency across a substantial portion of the housing market, which is critical for meeting climate targets.
reforms.source_quote: The government should reconsider and recommit to the minimum standard to band C with a concrete timeline to support the uprating of the overall housing stock.

reforms.description: The UK government introduced the Heat and Training Grant to support up to 10,000 training opportunities for heat pump and heat network installers from 2023-24 to 2024-25, addressing the skills gap in the green energy sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the skills of workers in the green energy sector, this initiative supports the transition to low-carbon technologies and improves the capacity for energy efficiency upgrades.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has launched the Heat and Training Grant to support training opportunities.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative addresses a critical skills gap in the green energy sector, which is essential for achieving the UK's decarbonisation goals.
reforms.source_quote: The government introduced in September 2023 the Heat and Training Grant to support up to 10,000 heat pump and heat network training opportunities from 2023-24 to 2024-25.

reforms.description: The UK government expanded the Boiler Upgrade Scheme in 2023, increasing the grant amount available per homeowner from GBP 5,000 to GBP 7,500 to support the installation of renewable heating systems.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing increased financial support for renewable heating installations, this scheme encourages the adoption of low-carbon technologies, contributing to the decarbonisation of homes.
reforms.status_evidence: The amount available per homeowner was increased and the overall size of the scheme was expanded.
reforms.importance_rationale: This expansion of an existing scheme significantly enhances support for renewable heating, which is crucial for achieving energy efficiency and carbon reduction targets.
reforms.source_quote: In 2023, the amount available per homeowner was increased from GBP 5,000 to GBP 7,500 and the overall size of the scheme was expanded.

reforms.description: The UK government is implementing a major expansion of childcare support to improve affordability, providing working parents with 30 hours of childcare per week for children aged nine months to five years, significantly increasing access compared to previous limits.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This reform is expected to increase employment by 114,000 full-time equivalents, thereby enhancing labor supply and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform is set to be fully rolled out with a phased approach starting in April 2024.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in childcare support, aiming to enhance labor market participation and address gender inequalities in the workforce.
reforms.source_quote: When fully rolled out, the reform will provide working parents with 30 hours of childcare per week for 38 weeks per year for each child aged nine months to five years in England.
